feuds:
Angie: Two-year ongoing conflict over accusations and actions Angie allegedly took against Meredith; Angie refuses to acknowledge wrongdoing while Meredith claims she apologized in Mexico
Mary Crosby: Tension over Mary calling the group 'not the friendliest,' with Meredith taking personal offense before partially conceding the point
Lisa Barlow: Residual friction over the Jen Shah phone call debate and broader group dynamics
key_moments:
Defended accepting Jen Shah's prison call: 'This isn't like we're going for a sleepover or something. This was a ten minute phone call.'
Pushed back on the group's zero-tolerance stance on Jen: 'So what do you think she should do? Just curl up in the corner and die?'
Acknowledged calling Heather a b*tch: 'Yes, I did say that. And I was heated, and it's a horrible word.'
Confirmed she apologized to Angie in Mexico, with corroboration from others on the couch
Ended the Mary exchange with a self-aware concession: 'So yes, Angie, maybe you're right. I am a cold b*tch. Because when people aren't nice to me, I am very cold.'
talking_points:
Forgiving Jen Shah was a personal choice for my own peace — I'm not co-signing her behavior, I picked up a phone
I apologized to Angie in Mexico; I need her to actually accept it and move forward rather than relitigating the same two years
The 'cold' label: own it strategically — warmth is earned, not owed to strangers two seconds into a relationship
Heather's husband repeatedly highlighting the b*tch comment felt like a pile-on; address the context, not just the word
Forgiveness is my value system — that applies to Jen, to Angie, to everyone — but forgiveness doesn't mean friendship
confessional_draft: I forgive people for me — not for them. Accepting Jen Shah's phone call doesn't mean I've forgotten what she did; it means I refuse to carry that weight forever. And if that makes me a 'cold b*tch' to some people in this room, honestly? I've heard worse.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds:
Meredith Marks: Disagreement over the Jen Shah phone call — Lisa would not accept Jen's call and is surprised Meredith did
Angie: Implicit tension as part of the group dynamics surrounding the Angie/Meredith conflict
key_moments:
Firmly stated she would never accept a call from Jen Shah: 'If Jen Shah called me, I would not accept her call. She tormented us.'
Drew a moral distinction between Meredith talking to Jen vs. Monica: 'She lied. She lied to all of you... she looked us in her eyes and convinced — I'm innocent.'
Pointed out Jen blamed her and Meredith for her going to prison: 'She said that you and I did something to make her go to prison. She did say that. She blamed us.'
talking_points:
Jen Shah lied to everyone's faces until the moment she legally couldn't — that's not a gray area, that's a character verdict
There's a meaningful difference between seeking out someone to dig dirt (Monica) and accepting a call from a convicted fraudster who blamed you for her crimes
Hold the line: some people go too far and Jen is one of them — that's not a lack of forgiveness, it's a boundary
Stay consistent: you're not attacking Meredith's choice, you're asserting your own
confessional_draft: Jen Shah looked me dead in the eyes and swore she was innocent — and then pleaded guilty the second it suited her. She blamed me for her going to prison. So no, I will not be on her call list, and honestly I'm a little shocked that anyone would be. That's not bitterness — that's self-respect.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds: Meredith Marks: Residual tension after Meredith called her a b*tch and Heather was removed from Meredith's event; Heather's husband repeatedly bringing up the comment added friction
key_moments:
Confirmed her name is NOT on Jen Shah's call list: 'No.' — a brief but pointed moment of alignment with the anti-Jen camp
Noted the unhealthiness of ongoing conflict: 'It's not healthy for us to continue this way.'
Expressed hurt that Meredith's husband kept highlighting her calling Meredith a b*tch after she was removed from Meredith's event
talking_points:
You've drawn a clean line on Jen Shah — your name isn't on the list, full stop
The b*tch comment came from a place of real hurt after being publicly removed from an event — context matters
You're playing peacemaker this reunion; lean into the 'let's move forward' energy without being a pushover
Address the husband dynamic — it felt like a pile-on and you deserve to say so
confessional_draft: I called Meredith a b*tch, and yeah, that was wrong — but I also got escorted out of her event in front of everyone. I was hurt. The word was heated, but the feeling behind it was real. I've moved on; I just wish everyone else's husbands would too.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds:
Meredith Marks: Mary called the group 'not the friendliest' and specifically called out Meredith and another castmate as 'pitiful' and 'cold,' sparking a tense exchange
Angie: Lumped together with Meredith in Mary's 'cold' assessment, creating a minor three-way tension
key_moments:
Called out the group on arrival: 'You guys are not the friendliest people' and specifically said Meredith and another cast member were 'pitiful'
Apologized in Mexico for the comment but stood by the observation in the reunion setting
Was told by Meredith: 'I've known you two seconds. Okay? I'm gonna need you to stop talking' — a pointed rebuke
Described Meredith as 'icy': 'You're icy, I mean. You're nice to me usually. And so I'm nice to you and warm.'
talking_points:
You walked into a tight-knit group and called it cold — own that it landed hard even if it was honest
The Mexico apology happened; make clear you meant it while also standing by the underlying observation
You're new here — pick your battles; 'icy' is a descriptor, not a declaration of war
Distinguish between critique of the group dynamic vs. a personal attack on any individual
confessional_draft: I walked into this group and told the truth — they weren't warm, and pretending otherwise wouldn't have been me. I apologized for how I said it, but I won't apologize for having eyes. Meredith herself admitted she's cold to people who aren't nice to her first — so maybe I wasn't that far off.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

key_moments:
Participated in the group debate about Jen Shah, noting Jen 'really just treated your kids and you really bad' when speaking to Meredith
Weighed in on the forgiveness discussion: 'Some people just go too far. And she's one of them.'
talking_points:
You were measured and empathetic in the Jen Shah debate — reinforce that position without piling on
You're not a central combatant this part of the reunion — use it to come across as the grounded voice in the room
Be ready to speak to your own relationship with forgiveness if the conversation turns back to you
confessional_draft: Forgiveness is beautiful — I believe in it. But there's a difference between forgiving someone for your own peace and inviting them back into your life like nothing happened. Jen Shah crossed a line that I don't think a ten-minute phone call from prison fixes.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City

feuds:
Meredith Marks: Two-year conflict where Angie allegedly came after Meredith repeatedly for things Meredith claims she didn't do; Angie accused of refusing to acknowledge her own behavior
Heather Gay: Indirect tension as Heather's removal from Meredith's event was linked back to Angie's role in stirring conflict
key_moments:
Challenged Meredith directly: 'Can you take any type of accountability?' — pushing for a confession Meredith resisted
Had her receipts debate reframed negatively: the group distinguished between 'digging dirt on friends' and Meredith's Jen call, with Angie's Monica situation used as the unflattering comparison
Was called out by Meredith: 'This all started from you coming after me for two years for things I didn't do. And you have done those things to me, and you refuse to acknowledge any.'
Was name-checked when Meredith conceded the 'cold b*tch' label: 'So yes, Angie, maybe you're right' — a backhanded acknowledgment of Angie's critique
talking_points:
You went looking for accountability from Meredith — make clear what specifically you needed her to own
The Monica receipts situation: reframe it as diligence, not dirt-digging — you were protecting the group
The Mexico apology happened and was witnessed — you need to publicly accept it and articulate what moving forward looks like
The 'cold b*tch' moment is actually a win for you — Meredith conceded your point; don't gloat, but don't walk away from it either
confessional_draft: I didn't come after Meredith for two years for fun — I came after the truth. And the fact that she finally admitted she can be cold, that she can be icy? Honey, that's all I was ever saying. I accept the apology, but let's be real — it took a full reunion to get here.
show: Real Housewives of Salt Lake City
